sections.heading: Childhood mortality rates were lower than in 2022
sections.body: In the 0-4 years before the survey, all listed childhood mortality rates were lower in 2025 than in 2022. Under-5 mortality decreased from 26 to 22 deaths per 1,000 live births, and infant mortality decreased from 22 to 18. Neonatal mortality went from 15 to 12, post-neonatal mortality from 7 to 6, and child mortality from 5 to 3. The declines were modest, but they appeared across the indicators instead of being limited to one age interval.

takeaways: In the 0-4 years before the survey, under-5 mortality was 22 deaths per 1,000 live births in 2025, down from 26 in 2022.
Infant mortality also went down, from 22 to 18 deaths per 1,000 live births.
In 2025, rates were lower than in 2022 for neonatal, post-neonatal, infant, child, and under-5 mortality.
